lyrics:
I was seventeen with a rust-red truck
Dust on my boots, heart full of luck
The summer sun lit the water gold
At Sawmill Pond, where secrets unfold

She showed up barefoot, hair like flame
Said her name soft - I never asked again
We dove in deep, both young and strong
Carving forever what never felt wrong

Sawmill Pond, where the world stood still
Time poured slow like the evening chill
Two shadows tangled on a drifting day
No map, no rules, just hearts at play
I loved her once in the wild and the warm
Down by the Sawmill Pond

We talked 'bout stars and leaving town
Built castles in mud just to watch 'em drown
She kissed like fire, left like rain
I never saw her face again

Sawmill Pond, where the echoes stay
In ripples of a long-lost day
The boy I was still haunts that shore
With her laugh caught in the open door
I loved her once in the wild and the warm
Down by the Sawmill Pond

Now I'm older, lines in my skin
Still hear her voice when the night drifts in
Regret is gentle, but it never lets go
That summer when our hearts burned hot and slow

Sawmill Pond, in the hush of dusk
Memory's ghost in a pickup truck
Two kids chasing what they couldn't keep
Woke something in me I can never repeat
I loved her once in the wild and the warm
Down by the Sawmill Pond

Yeah, I loved her once
At Sawmill Pond
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track features a warm, dynamic texture that shifts from sparse, acoustic-led storytelling in the verses to a wide, anthemic rock soundscape in the choruses, creating a deeply nostalgic and bittersweet atmosphere. The energy builds significantly throughout, moving from a gentle, reflective feel to a driving, passionate release that feels both powerful and liberating. The production is polished and spacious, allowing the dense layers of electric guitar and powerful drums in the choruses to feel expansive without overwhelming the clear, earnest lead vocal. The song is defined by these dramatic dynamic shifts, which effectively mirror the theme of a quiet memory bursting with intense, youthful emotion.

review.review_text: PCBender writes the kind of summer-memory song that earns its nostalgia by earning its details first: a rust-red truck, a girl who "kissed like fire, left like rain," a mud castle built just to watch it drown. In E major and moving at a brisk clip, the track opens spare and acoustic before the chorus opens up into a wide, drums-forward rock swell that lands somewhere between John Mellencamp's heartland tenderness and early Tom Petty urgency. The production is smart enough to let those dynamic swings do the heavy lifting, so when the final chorus hits, it feels like the memory itself finally breaking the surface. A highlight, and one of his most complete songs.
anchors_used: lyric: kissed like fire, left like rain
lyric: built castles in mud just to watch em drown
